title: lolium temulentum f. laeve
intro: Lolium temulentum f. laeve, also known as smooth-dock or wild oats, is a plant species that belongs to the grass family and is characterized by its smooth leaves and seed heads. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to reduce swelling and protect against cell damage. The health benefits associated with Lolium temulentum f. laeve include its potential to support digestive health, reduce stress and anxiety, and improve sleep quality. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and its extracts are also used in supplements and topical applications.
